Document:
# Security Officer Ensuring the safety of both station and crew is number one for [color=#cb0000]Security[/color]. They are ready for any situation, and [color=#cb0000]Security[/color] are on hand to maintain order, control crowds, and do general deductive work.
They face other troupes, like [textlink="traitors" link="Traitors"], as well as unruly [textlink="crew" link="Crew"]--though nothing is stopping other officers from being the traitors or unruly crew.
As a Security Officer, it would do you well to familiarize yourself with the many [textlink="masks" link="TroupesAndMasks"] in the game, and understand what excuses someone may use and when, in order to make the most accurate deduction.
## Gear First we have non-lethals--a step above simply telling someone to cooperate with instructions. Stunbatons can be used to incapacitate targets at close range, and handcuffs can be applied to deny a criminal free movement and access to their hands. Cuffs will also shove the person down if your [color=red]harm mode[/color] is active during uncuff.
## Flashes [color=#a4885c]Flashes[/color] are an effective tool for dispersing crowds and apprehending particularly evasive targets, with a large area of effect blinding all those without protection. It is worth noting that flashes can be both used in a large area ([color=yellow]Z[/color] hotkey) and if you attack somebody with it while [color=red]harm mode[/color] is active. Important to note that localized flashes last [color=yellow]2.5[/color] times as long as large area ones. [color=#cb0000]Security[/color] and [color=#a4885c]some[/color] other members of staff have eye protection from flashbangs and flashes.
